title: pcf-delta: The growth-law correction constant δ = log R∞ of the quadratic polynomial continued fraction — structure, a closed-form bracket, and certified high-precision values
description:
A characterization of the additive correction constant δ = log R∞ > 0 that the companion growth-law note introduces and leaves undetermined for the quadratic polynomial continued fraction V(A,B,C) = 1 + Kn≥1 1/(An2+Bn+C), integers A,B,C ≥ 1. With bk = Ak2+Bk+C, the standard continuant q0=1, q1=b1, qn=bnqn-1+qn-2, scaled partial quotients un = 1/(bn-1bn) and S = ∑n≥2 un, the scaled limit is R∞ = lim qn/∏k≤n bk and δ = log R∞.

Contributions. (i) R∞ is exactly the independence polynomial of the path graph on the indices, giving the elementary two-sided bracket log(1+S) ≤ δ ≤ −log(1−S). (ii) Exact closed forms for the cluster sums σ2, σ3 in terms of power sums of un, machine-verified against brute-force enumeration of non-consecutive subsets to residuals ~10-54. (iii) Certified high-precision values of δ for nine triples (≥39 correct digits each, ≥44 for (1,0,1): δ(1,0,1) = 0.12385719436062639272850498970259084096757955…) via an exact downward recurrence seeded by an order-3 cluster expansion at large M, with an independent forward + Neville cross-check (~25 digits) and an exact confluent-polygamma seed for degenerate (repeated-pole) triples such as (1,2,1) where S = π2/3 − 13/4. (iv) A rigorous null: no low-height integer relation is found for V, R∞, or δ (mpmath identify at 100 digits; PSLQ over {1, π, log 2, γ, Catalan, ζ(3)}), and a precision-stability test shows the high-height PSLQ vectors are working-precision artifacts, not genuine relations. (v) A machine-checked finitary core in Lean 4 / Mathlib (toolchain pinned lean4 v4.30.0, Mathlib v4.30.0): the Casoratian (discrete-Wronskian) identity and the existence of R∞ = lim Rn by monotone-bounded convergence, with clean axiom cones.

Epistemic status (SIARC four-class). PROVEN — the Lean core (Casoratian identity; existence of R∞), each declaration verified by #print axioms to a subset of {propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound} with no sorryAx and no sorry/admit in source. STRUCTURAL — the scaled recurrence and tail estimate, the independence-polynomial identity, the bracket, and the closed forms for S, σ2, σ3 (complete elementary hand proofs, not yet formalized). VERIFIED — the cluster identities and all tabulated δ values numerically (mpmath), with the bracket satisfied on every tabulated triple. CONJECTURED — δ has no low-height elementary closed form and is a new transcendental constant of the family; irrationality of δ is open. A null integer-relation result is a complete and honest answer, not a gap.
notes: ORCID (0009-0000-6192-8273) is source-verified. PARENT DEPENDENCY (isSupplementTo): the companion growth-law note, concept (cite-all) DOI 10.5281/zenodo.20564681, which introduces delta and leaves it undetermined; this DOI was verified live (doi.org 302 -> zenodo.org/records/20565409) on 2026-06-07. CONTEXT (references): the deposited ladder paper, concept DOI 10.5281/zenodo.19491767, verified live. GATE STATUS at draft time: (1) parent and ladder DOIs both resolve [confirmed]; (2) the Lean package lean/pcf_continuant was machine-rechecked with 'lake env lean Check.lean' (toolchain leanprover/lean4:v4.30.0) on 2026-06-07 -- all twelve declarations report axiom cones that are subsets of {propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}, no sorryAx, and Basic.lean contains no sorry/admit [confirmed, not asserted from memory]; (3) UNRESOLVED operator decision: whether the ten reproducibility scripts (harness_rinf.py, continuant_verify.py, S_closed_form.py, S_confluent.py, verify_clusters.py, hp_delta_v2.py, finalize_m2.py, verify_forward_crosscheck.py, hp_probe.py, pslq_stability.py), the data file delta_values_m2.txt, and the Lean package ship inside this deposit or in a linked public repository. LICENSE: CC-BY-4.0 for the paper unit (delta_characterization.pdf); the bundled code (the scripts and the Lean package lean/pcf_continuant) is Apache-2.0 per the project convention. DLMF is cited at section level only (sec 5.15 polygamma, sec 5.7 series) -- no equation numbers are asserted. The non-elementarity claim is CONJECTURED, supported by a precision-stable null, not proven. DISCIPLINE: v1.0 was OPERATOR-GATED and DEPOSITED on Zenodo 2026-06-07 (version DOI 10.5281/zenodo.20578401, concept (cite-all) DOI 10.5281/zenodo.20578400). THIS FILE IS v1.1 METADATA: NOT YET DEPOSITED -- the v1.1 version DOI is to be minted at the operator-gated v1.1 release and is not asserted here. v1.1 supersedes v1.0 (frozen in archive/) and adds Appendix A (an inclusion-exclusion hand derivation of the cluster closed forms sigma_2, sigma_3) plus a reorganized repository layout (scripts under src/, Lean under lean/, single-source METADATA.yml, tools/validate_metadata.py, operator-gated tools/hooks/pre-push). Public GitHub repo https://github.com/papanokechi/pcf-delta; no GitHub release was cut and the Zenodo webhook is OFF. This .zenodo.json is documentation only and was not used to trigger a release; metadata was entered by hand at publish. The parent note 10.5281/zenodo.20564681 was confirmed deposited and is the intended isSupplementTo target.
